pattern: Since the two smallest pizzas in any group of four never contribute anything, the smallest half of all pizzas should be spent purely as filler while the largest half is split between odd-day maximums and even-day second-maximums.
hints: In any group of four, the two lightest pizzas never add to your gained weight -- only the heaviest (odd days) or second heaviest (even days) matters, so the lightest half of all pizzas overall should just be used as disposable filler.
Sort the pizzas from heaviest to lightest. The very top slice, one pizza per odd-numbered day, should be taken directly as pure gains -- each one alone maximizes what an odd day can contribute.

For the even-numbered days, pair up the next-heaviest pizzas two at a time (in sorted order) and take the lighter one of each pair as that day's gain -- pairing large values together protects the 'second heaviest' from being pulled down by a small filler.

edge_inputs.description: minimum size, a single odd day only
an odd number of days forces one more odd day than even day
one huge outlier weight tempts a naive per-group max grab

stress.description: random weights with size a multiple of four
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(4, min(200, size))
    n -= n % 4
    n = max(4, n)
    pizzas = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [pizzas]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maxWeight(self, pizzas):
        n = len(pizzas)
        arr = sorted(pizzas, reverse=True)
        num_days = n // 4
        odd_days = (num_days + 1) // 2
        even_days = num_days // 2
        total = sum(arr[:odd_days])
        for i in range(even_days):
            total += arr[odd_days + 2 * i + 1]
        return total
